action.reasoning: Observation: The baseline model achieved a very low MSE (val_loss = 0.004) but a massively negative R^2 for specific humidity tendencies (r2_ptend_q = -22 billion). Analysis: This happens because the uppermost levels of specific humidity tendency are exactly zero in the dataset (variance = 0). Predicting even tiny non-zero values for these variables results in an infinite/huge negative R^2 penalty when evaluated in normalized space. Decision: I will compute the variance of the targets and create a mask to force the model's predictions to exactly 0 for zero-variance variables. I will also upgrade the model to a Pre-LN Residual MLP (1024 hidden dim, 6 blocks) to improve capacity, and train for 5 epochs.
